Pt presents for adj visit. Torque of U laterals, esp UR2 looked off and on palpation, permanent cuspids seem a bit mesial. Checked initial xrays and cuspid position on UL was close to root of lateral and lateral already has short root. Discussed with [DOCTOR] and decided best plan is to ext Ucs at this time and DB appliances at next apt. Informed pts mom that due to proximity of permanent cuspid to lateral root, we need to extract primary to prevent any complications and correct path of eruption. Mom is aware that there is a chance that there may have been some damage to lateral root. Also informed her that the patient will likely need a second phase after eruption of all permanent teeth and that he will be on recall over the next few years. Did some detailing bends and will remove appliances and take final records in 4 weeks. Will fabricate U Dugoni retainer and following pts trip to Mexico next month, will get back on track with HG. Gave pt referral for ext #c and #h to be done as soon as pt is able to get apt. Will get iCat as part of final records to eval position of permanent cuspids. To prevent lateral movement of U2s, will fabricate Dugoni retainer with some holding wires distal to U2s.

Clinic:
Ortho initial exam Patient arrived with mother. Mother reports that patient has autism. No medications. No allergies Patient visits a general dentist regularly at a local hospital. Had a initial exam with special care at UOP due to history of being uncooperative. However, patient was cooperative through exam and impressions. M/C positions: right class 2/1, left class 3/3 LR7 not yet unerupted. UR5 lingual displaced out of arch. Right posterior crossbite. May begin with upper arch first to gauge how comfortable it is for patient. Major crowding issue with upper arch. Some functional issues with bite and mouth breathing habit and thumb sucking. Patient has autism. Patient scored above 25 on HRD Took impressions, max and mand. Patient very cooperative. Treatment recommended. We will consider begining with upper arch only and determine how the patient responds to treatment.

Yes Covering Faculty: [doctor] Chief Concern: " "Upper left premolars are coming in crooked and there is spacing of the lower front teeth" " Reviewed MedHx/Signigicant findings: Y/NSMH Reviewed DentHx/Significant findings: Y/Last dental checkup June 2017 EOE: Facial Profile: Convex Midline: Upper: Coincident with facial midline, Lower: 1mm to the right CR/CO Shift: affected by UL3 in crossbite, should resolve when crossbite is resolved. IOE: Arch Analysis: Maxillary Adequate, Mandibular slight spacing UL4, UL5 rotated almost 90 degrees (BL cusps positioned MD in arch) UL 3 in crossbite OJ: 6mm OB: 4mm R Molar Classification: 2 L Molar Classification: 1 TMJ: ( No ) Crepitus, ( No ) , No Clicking of TMJ, Pain on Palpation, ( No ) Hx of Trauma Recommendations: Yes Records to be taken, CBCT ( Full + Report ) , IO and EO photographs, Scan/Models of Maxillary and Mandibular Arches. Explained to patient that braces could fix the two major concerns of the rotated upper premolars and the spacing of the lower incisors. Treatment may take 24+ months. Now they will get a quote from the treatment coordinator, and the next steps include getting records before starting treatment. Patient was curious if extraction of the primary teeth sooner could have prevented such rotations, and we explained that the premolars could have just been coming in that way, so it is uncertain if that would have actually helped. Patient and mother understood. NV: Consultation of Tx Options, Begin Tx upon approval

Retainers may not fit but we can try. Other option is to mail retainers but if they do not fit then we can't help. Pt elected to call back on Nov 9th to update on what she will decide to do.

original_note:
Clinic:
OR [faculty]Patient presents for retainer check. Patient arrived on tme: Yes Minor accompanied by: Mom on cell phone. Pt. is not a minor Faculty Assigned: * Covering faculty: [doctor] Patient had U and L essix delivered by [doctor] on 01/2021. Patient states that these retainers were worn until 1 W ago but that they were thrown away by her cousin. From the evaluation on 01/2021, it appears that the patient did not wear the retainer as there are spaces between the U 3/3. Assessment today: Patient has spacing on the U 3/3 with 100% deep bite and 6 mm of OJ. The patient's chief complaint is that she has spacing between her upper teeth and her deep bite. She stated that she is happy with the alignment of her lower teeth and does not want to do treatment on this arch. I explained to the Mom (on the phone ordering McDonalds on speaker) that the patient is a candidate for comprehensive or limited treatment. If the patient wants to resolve her deep bite, overjet, and upper incisor flaring, she will need comprehensive treatment. However, the patient stated that she only wants to treat her upper arch. In this case, I explained to the patient that she could do limited treatment with an upper 2x6 & powerchain which would close the UA anterior spacing. However, it was explained to the patient that this would NOT resolve her deep bite or her overjet. The patient and Mom understood. The limitations of limited treatment will be explained to the patient and her Mom prior to signing the contract. It was also recommended to the patient that her wisdom teeth should be extracted (As per her final pano). This recommendation was made on the appearance of anLL8 and parially erupted/partiallyLR8. The patient was given a referral letter to give to her general dentist to assess the 3rd molars for possible 3rd molar extractions. The pt. stated that she did not have a general dentist. The patient was explained options at the school for a general dentist or recommended to find another outside dentist. It was explained to the patient that she needs dental clearance before starting treatment. Pt. was given records release form to release her pano/ other radiographs if she would like for her dentist. Patient and Mom understood all information and agreed. NV : Records
